parsed_review.summary: CP00-774 descriptor-only Migration Platform permission-matrix-primary pack passes hardened read-only review: consistent binding/requirements/service/validator/test/contract chain, all required validations report exit 0, no-write/no-real-data and authority boundaries intact, review hardening posture sound. One cosmetic P3 in an auxiliary scope artifact; no P0/P1. Recommend closeout.

findings.title: changed-file-scope lists this pack's own untracked artifact directories under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved
findings.details: The unrelated_dirty_files_preserved array (lines 205-222) includes docs/closeout-packs/cp00-774/ (line 211) and artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-774/ (line 207), which are this pack's own required evidence and review directories and are also correctly enumerated in active_pack_required_untracked_files (lines 19-36). The authoritative manifest's worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (manifest.json:217-230) correctly excludes these and lists only genuinely unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, workbook docs, new-session-handoff.md, c-ldip artifact, docx). Staging uses active_pack_required_untracked_files, so no incorrect files would be staged. This is a denormalized/loose categorization in an auxiliary review-hardening artifact, not a pack defect.
findings.recommendation: Optionally have the scope generator subtract active_pack_required_untracked_files (and the active review dir) from changed-file-scope.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved so the two lists are mutually exclusive and less confusing. No code or staging change required for closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking, cosmetic. The authoritative manifest categorization is correct and staging is driven by active_pack_required_untracked_files, so closeout integrity is unaffected. Safe to close CP00-774.
